title: Inside the Whale
description: On pages 13–16, inside the whale as the Straw Hats collide with Laboon, a gigantic whale guarding the entrance to the Grand Line, and Luffy is separated inside it.
scene: Inside the Whale. The Straw Hats collide with Laboon, a gigantic whale guarding the entrance to the Grand Line, and Luffy is separated inside it. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Damn it! Planning to dive under the sea!?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Wait!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I said wait! Give me back my crew- mates!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: We were just about to start our grand adventure!! They're my important friends!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: I have a feeling of a comment

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Huh?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: What do you make of this?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: What do I make of this...?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Yeah, what's your explanation for this...

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I could've sworn we were Swal- lowered up by a whale but...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Are we just dreaming or something?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: ...yeah, this has got to be a dream...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: then...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: What's with that house on that island?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Must be an illusion...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Then what about this?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: It's a giant squid!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Seems like there's a person here.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Let's hope it's a person.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: B-By the way, where did Luffy go...?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I've had enough... I wanna go home...

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on inside the whale. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
